summary: Structured legal research from question formulation through precedent analysis to attorney review packet.

inputs.name: research_question
inputs.description: The legal question or issue to research.

inputs.name: source_constraints
inputs.description: Preferred sources, time limits, or budget constraints.

outputs.name: research_findings
outputs.description: Structured findings organized by legal issue.

outputs.name: precedent_analysis
outputs.description: Analysis of relevant precedents and their applicability.

outputs.name: attorney_review_packet
outputs.description: Summary with citations for attorney verification.

budget.notes: High-stakes workflow. Keep issue spotting and human review explicit.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is legal research and not legal advice.
Require defined research question and jurisdiction scope.
Cite all sources and verify jurisdiction applicability.
End with attorney review packet; do not present conclusions as legal advice.

steps.action: Refine the research question and identify search parameters.
Search case law, statutes, and regulations for relevant authorities.
Analyze found precedents for applicability and weight.
Draft structured research summary with proper citations.
Compile attorney review packet with findings and open questions.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read documents, policies, and reference materials.

verification.method: Check the output states the research question and scope.
Check all findings include proper citations.
Check output includes attorney review packet with open questions.

success_criteria: Research question is clearly defined and scoped.
All findings include proper citations with jurisdiction notes.
Output ends with attorney review packet, not legal conclusions.

rollback.when: If scope, confidentiality, or review authority is unclear, revert the draft and regenerate a narrower output for counsel review.
